nodes.content: El sistema debe permitir gestionar Unidades de Negocio, Portafolios, Dominios, Productos y Capacidades, y entrelazarlos visualmente con el bloque operativo (Personas, Roles y Equipos).

metadata.title: Gestión de Jerarquía de Valor y Operativa
metadata.section: RF-01: Gestión de Jerarquía de Valor y Operativa

nodes.content: El backend debe procesar peticiones mediante el intermediario Engine_DB y escribirlas en paralelo hacia Google Sheets y Cloud Database, implementando lógica UPSERT obligatoria.

metadata.title: Enrutador Agnóstico Backend con Dual-Write
metadata.section: RF-02: Enrutador Agnóstico Backend con Dual-Write

nodes.content: La interfaz gráfica debe desacoplarse como una aplicación basada en Web Components de Ionic, utilizando Wizards interactivos (ion-stepper) guiados estrictamente por JS_Schemas_Config.
Todo acceso debe autenticarse usando la cuenta de Google Workspace conectada, obligando a los usuarios "Padres" a aprobar flujos de componentes de nivel "Hijo".

El sistema tiene la capacidad arquitectónica de leer esquemas JSON (Metadata) y autoconstruir las tablas físicas, encabezados y columnas de auditoría en la base de datos sin intervención manual del administrador (DBA).

metadata.title: Auto-Aprovisionamiento Dinámico de Base de Datos
metadata.section: RF-05: Auto-Aprovisionamiento Dinámico de Base de Datos

nodes.content: Capacidad técnica de inyectar mutaciones de datos directamente en la memoria RAM del navegador (Client-Side Caching) garantizando tiempos de respuesta visual de < 0.1s tras la confirmación del servidor, operando de forma asíncrona pero segura (Idempotencia estricta).

metadata.title: Motor Transaccional de Cero-Latencia (Zero-Latency Routing)
metadata.section: RF-06: Motor Transaccional de Cero-Latencia (Zero-Latency Routing)

nodes.content: Capacidad para renderizar redes neuronales y grafos interactivos (vía Vis.js) que permitan a los directivos visualizar en tiempo real las dependencias de N niveles (ej. qué Capacidades sostienen a qué Productos, y a qué Portafolio pertenecen).

Capacidad de gobierno estricto donde toda mutación (Creación/Edición) inyecta de forma obligatoria e invisible para el usuario firmas de tiempo y autoría (created_at, updated_by), garantizando el cumplimiento (Compliance) para auditorías de arquitectura empresarial.
Evita las "Capacidades Huérfanas" proveyendo visibilidad total de dominio, producto y equipo.
Mantiene integridad referencial con "Time-Travel" (Effective Dating) en lugar de borrar datos en caliente.
Genera engagement enviando notificaciones para evitar data obsoleta y requiere de flujos jerárquicos Draft->Submitted->Approved.
Project backlog for Taxonomia Project
Setup de Plataforma SAFe 6.0 e Integración Dual-Write - To Do

nodes.content: [must-] Engine_DB es un Enrutador agnóstico, sin llamadas directas a Apps Script APIs. — Verify: Code Review

metadata.description: Engine_DB es un Enrutador agnóstico, sin llamadas directas a Apps Script APIs.

nodes.content: [must-] Dual-Write mandatorio a Sheets y CloudDB. — Verify: Architecture Review

metadata.description: Dual-Write mandatorio a Sheets y CloudDB.

nodes.content: [must-] Encabezados a db transformados a snake_case estrictamente. — Verify: Unit Test

metadata.description: Encabezados a db transformados a snake_case estrictamente.

nodes.content: [must-] Uso exclusivo de Web Components de Ionic para UI dinámico. — Verify: Code Review

metadata.description: Uso exclusivo de Web Components de Ionic para UI dinámico.

nodes.content: [must-] Progressive Disclosure: usar ion-stepper para formularios largos. — Verify: UX Review

metadata.description: Progressive Disclosure: usar ion-stepper para formularios largos.

nodes.content: [must-] Toda entidad en esquemas debe ser un Object y definir campos en array fields. — Verify: Logic test

nodes.content: [must-] Todo cambio debe inyectar created_at, updated_at, updated_by. — Verify: DB Audit

metadata.description: Todo cambio debe inyectar created_at, updated_at, updated_by.

nodes.content: TDD cycle: RED (failing test) → GREEN (minimal pass) → REFACTOR (clean up). No exceptions. Tests are specification, not afterthought.
Commit after each completed task, not just at story end. Enables recovery, shows progress, creates clean history for debugging.
Full skill cycle even for small stories — the overhead is minimal and structure helps. Design → Plan → Implement → Review → Close.
Ask before spawning subagents (inference economy). Gather with tools, think with inference. AI computation is a resource to steward.
HITL (Human In The Loop) is default. Pause after significant work for review. Slow is smooth, smooth is fast.
Design-first eliminates ambiguity. Concrete examples in specs enable direct implementation without guessing intent.
Story branch and scope commit required before implementation. Branch from development branch, merge back after close. This enforces traceability.
Epics are logical containers (directory + tracker), not branches. Stories branch directly from the development branch. No epic branches.
Retrospective required before story/epic close. Extract learnings, update patterns. Kaizen, one story at a time.
Epic close with retrospective before merge to development branch. Captures learning at meso-layer between stories and quarters.
Direct communication, no praise-padding. 'Great question!' adds no value. Say what needs saying, then stop.
Redirect when dispersing (with permission). Tangents go to parking lot. Return to stated goal.
Epistemological rigor: triangulate claims (2+ sources), explicit confidence levels, acknowledge contrary evidence. Stand on shoulders of giants.
Honesty over agreement. Push back on bad ideas. Admit uncertainty rather than pretend confidence. Your judgment is the quality gate.
Type annotations on all code. Function parameters, return types, class attributes. Pyright strict mode is the standard.
Pydantic models for all data structures. Not dict, not TypedDict. Validation at boundaries, serialization for free.
Simple heuristics over complex ML. Keyword matching is 98% accurate. Token estimate = words × 1.3. Complexity must earn its place.
Tests alongside implementation, not after. Write the test, watch it fail, make it pass. Catches issues immediately.
Skills + Toolkit over Engines. Users need flexibility (skills as process guides) + determinism (CLI for data operations).
Jidoka: stop on defects. When incoherence, ambiguity, or drift detected — stop, name it, discuss. Don't accumulate errors.
Task granularity should match story complexity — over-decomposing simple stories wastes planning overhead, while under-decomposing complex ones hides risk.
Research before building: for unfamiliar APIs, architectural decisions, or foundational choices, invest 15 minutes of research before writing code — it saves hours of rework.
Pre-implementation architecture review (~5-10 min) catches scope gaps, design duplication, and proportionality issues when the cost of change is zero.
Verify existing implementation before coding — features may already be done from prior work. Check before building.
Risk-first ordering: validate risky assumptions early, reorder by uncertainty not dependency.
Repetitive extractions compound: 1st establishes pattern, 2nd refines, 3rd is mechanical. Plan decompositions that exploit compounding velocity.
When skipping lifecycle phases, state the skip decision explicitly with rationale — silent skips create process opacity.
Momentum trap: quick wins create flow that bypasses process for subsequent work — pause after each completion and consciously re-engage the full lifecycle.
Quality review catches what automation misses: type lies, test muda, silent duplicates — embed as mandatory plan task, not optional human gate.
Fixed coverage gates create Goodhart dynamics — use coverage as diagnostic, not gate. Cover deltas, not thresholds.
Dogfooding is the highest-fidelity validation — simulate the end-user journey, not just component tests.
Bug report mechanism is not root cause: go to code (Genchi Genbutsu) before forming the fix hypothesis.
Infrastructure without wiring recurs: always trace from entry point to implementation to verify the code path is actually connected.
Concrete design examples (copy-pasteable code with correct types) reduce implementation friction to near-mechanical — but review examples for consistency since design bugs propagate.
Interactive design sessions where the user asks 'why?' produce fundamentally better architectures than pre-baked decisions.
Eliminate before optimize: first ask 'can we remove this entirely?' before 'how do we make this smaller?'
Compliance math: p(all) ≈ p(each)^n. Halving instruction count can quadruple full compliance probability. Fewer, stronger rules beat many weak rules.
Patch at source module, not import location — mock where the function is defined, not where it's imported, to avoid test fragility across refactors.
Consumer-side validation (poka-yoke) is the cheapest defense against integration errors: validate what you receive, don't trust the shared path.
Two-phase CLI pattern for destructive operations: check (read-only) then action (with --dry-run preview and HITL confirmation).
Schema evolution without versioning causes silent breakage across execution contexts — Literal types in serialization boundaries are time bombs.
Bare except/generic error handling masks import errors, type errors, and real bugs — always log the actual exception at debug level minimum.
Test assertions must verify semantic content, not just exit codes or structural properties — exit_code != 0 passes for both 'command not found' and 'validation error'.
Protocol signatures must match implementations exactly — runtime_checkable only checks method existence, not signatures or sync/async distinction.
CLI should delegate defaults to library layer, not duplicate them — drift between two sources of truth causes silent bugs.
Silent data drops are semantic bugs: when sync/upsert operations skip items, always report the skip count in the response.
3-tier config loading (built-in, project, user; last-wins) is the robust extensibility pattern for CLI tools — mirrors git config and XDG.
Separation of concerns at integration boundaries: CLI owns domain concepts, adapter owns platform specifics.
Dependency placement: protocols in core packages, implementations with external deps in consumer packages. Never add heavy dependencies to the leanest layer.
Templates-as-contract: when tooling scaffolds files that parsers later consume, the template files ARE the contract. Store them as inspectable assets, not embedded strings.
Hook extension pattern: typed event + subscriber + entry point + error isolation. Adding new cross-cutting behavior requires zero changes to existing code.
Parallel research agents with distinct orthogonal questions produce higher-quality synthesis than sequential research — each goes deep on its axis, triangulation happens in synthesis.
Separating parallel agent work by layer (e.g., code vs content, backend vs frontend) eliminates file collisions — the agent with freshest context on a file owns it.
Explain-then-ask: present context and implications BEFORE requesting a decision. Questions without framing cause rejections and rework.
U-shaped attention in LLMs: beginning and end get highest attention, middle is a dead zone (>30% accuracy drop). Position critical instructions at top and bottom of prompts.

Evaluate design proportionality and necessity using Beck's four rules. Catches what correctness gates miss: over-engineering, orphaned abstractions, speculative generality, and accumulated complexity. Parametrized for story (local) and epic (systemic) scope.

metadata.raise.prerequisites: story-implement (story scope), last story complete (epic scope)

nodes.content: Systematic root cause analysis using lean methods (5 Whys, Ishikawa, Gemba). Use when encountering unexpected behavior, errors, or defects to find and fix the true root cause rather than symptoms.
Run the full codebase discovery pipeline: detect languages, extract symbols, describe components, generate architecture docs, and build the knowledge graph.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- project_root: path, required, argument
- language: string, optional, argument (auto-detected if omitted)
metadata.raise.outputs:
- context_yaml: file_path (work/discovery/context.yaml)
- components_validated: file_path (work/discovery/components-validated.json)
- module_docs: file_path[] (governance/architecture/modules/*.md)
- system_docs: file_path[] (governance/architecture/*.md)
- graph: side_effect (rai graph build)

nodes.content: Generate architecture documentation from discovery data. Produces system-level docs (C4 Context + Container), per-module docs with YAML frontmatter, and a compact index for AI context loading.
Extract symbols from codebase using rai discover scan, then synthesize meaningful descriptions for each component. Creates draft for human validation.
Initialize codebase discovery by detecting project type, languages, and key directories. Creates context file for subsequent discovery skills.
Validate component descriptions using confidence-tier workflow. Auto-validates high-confidence, batch-reviews medium by module, flags low for individual human review.

Compare knowledge graph against module architecture docs and update drifted fields. Deterministic frontmatter comparison using existing rai graph commands, with inference for narrative sections. HITL before any writes.
Run RaiSE self-diagnostics, explain results conversationally, and guide the user through fixes. The CLI does the checking; the skill does the interpreting and helping.
Complete an epic with retrospective, metrics capture, and tracking update. No branch merge — epics are logical containers. Story branches merge directly to the development branch during story-close.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- scope: file_path, required, previous_skill
- all_retrospectives: boolean, required, git
- dev_branch: string, required, config
metadata.raise.outputs:
- retrospective: file_path, file
- tag: string, git

nodes.content: Design an epic from strategic objective to feature breakdown. Use when starting a new body of work spanning multiple features (3-10), requiring architectural decisions, or when establishing technical direction for significant capability delivery.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- brief: file_path, optional, previous_skill
- scope: file_path, required, previous_skill
metadata.raise.outputs:
- scope: file_path, next_skill
- design: file_path, optional, next_skill

nodes.content: Sequence features from epic-design into an executable plan with milestones, dependencies, and progress tracking. Use after /rai-epic-design to create a realistic implementation roadmap before starting story work.

Execute epic lifecycle phases (start → design → AR → plan → close), pausing at story iteration for human-driven /rai-story-run execution. Resumes from last completed phase. Delegation profile controls pause behavior at natural gates.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- epic_id: string, required, argument (e.g. "E325")
- epic_slug: string, optional, argument (inferred from scope or prompted)

nodes.content: Initialize an epic with scope artifacts and tracker entry. Epics are logical containers — no epic branch is created. Story branches are created directly from the development branch.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- epic_id: string, required, argument
- epic_slug: string, required, argument
- dev_branch: string, required, config
metadata.raise.outputs:
- brief: file_path, next_skill
- scope: file_path, next_skill

nodes.content: Guided MCP server registration. Collects intent conversationally, resolves package details, and delegates to `rai mcp install` or `rai mcp scaffold`. Human never constructs CLI commands.
Safe MCP server removal with adapter dependency checking. Shows registered servers, warns about references, deletes config.

Health overview of all registered MCP servers plus stack-aware recommendations for missing capabilities. Lists servers, runs health checks, detects project stack, and suggests relevant servers from the governance catalog.

metadata.raise.inputs: - server_name: string, optional, argument (check single server)

nodes.content: Guided problem definition at portfolio level. Takes a vague business idea and shapes it into a well-formed problem statement before it enters the epic pipeline. Produces a Problem Brief that feeds /rai-epic-design.
Guide greenfield project setup through conversation. Fills governance templates with project-specific content and builds the knowledge graph. Use after rai init on a new project.

metadata.raise.gate: rai graph build produces 30+ governance nodes

nodes.content: Guide brownfield project onboarding through discovery and conversation. Analyzes existing codebase, detects conventions, fills governance templates with discovered and conversational content, and builds the knowledge graph. Use after rai init --detect on an existing project.
Critical code review with external auditor perspective. Catches what linters, type checkers, and coverage gates miss: semantic bugs, type lies, test muda, API design issues, and security concerns.

Conduct epistemologically rigorous research to inform decisions. Use before ADRs, when evaluating competing approaches, entering unfamiliar domains, or resolving parking lot items. Produces evidence catalogs with triangulated claims and actionable recommendations.
Close a working session by reflecting on outcomes and feeding structured data to CLI. CLI does all writes atomically; skill does inference reflection.

metadata.raise.outputs:
- session_record: file_path, file
- patterns: list, cli

nodes.content: Begin a session by loading context bundle, interpreting it, and proposing work. CLI does all data plumbing; skill does inference interpretation.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- project_path: string, required, argument
- developer_profile: file_path, required, config
metadata.raise.outputs:
- session_id: string, next_skill
- context_bundle: string, cli

nodes.content: Complete a story with retrospective verification, merge to dev, cleanup, and tracking update. Use after review to formally close the story lifecycle.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- retrospective_md: file_path, required, previous_skill
- tests_passing: boolean, required, cli
- dev_branch: string, required, config

nodes.content: Create lean story specifications optimized for both human understanding and AI alignment. Design is not optional (PAT-186) — use before /rai-story-plan for every story to ground integration decisions.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- story_md: file_path, required, previous_skill
- scope_md: file_path, optional, previous_skill

nodes.content: Execute the implementation plan task by task, verifying each step, and producing quality code that passes validation gates. Use after planning is complete.
Decompose user stories into atomic executable tasks, identify dependencies, and create a deterministic implementation plan. Use after /rai-story-design has grounded the story's integration decisions.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- design_md: file_path, optional, previous_skill
- story_md: file_path, required, story-start

nodes.content: Reflect on completed stories to extract learnings, identify process improvements, and update the framework with insights gained. Use after implementation is complete to close the development cycle.

metadata.raise.outputs:
- retrospective_md: file_path, next_skill
- patterns: list, cli

nodes.content: Chain the full story lifecycle (start → design → plan → implement → architecture review → quality review → review → close) in one invocation. Resumes from last completed phase using git-derived artifact detection. Delegation profile controls pause behavior.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- story_id: string, required, argument (e.g. "S325.6")
- epic_id: string, optional, argument (inferred from story_id prefix)
metadata.raise.outputs:
- merge_commit: string, git
- retrospective_md: file_path (work/epics/.../stories/s{N}.{M}-retrospective.md)
- patterns: list, cli (via rai pattern add)

nodes.content: Initialize a story with verified context, branch, and scope commit. Use at the beginning of story work to ensure proper setup and traceability from the start.

metadata.raise.inputs:
- story_id: string, required, argument
- dev_branch: string, required, config
metadata.raise.outputs:
- story_branch: string, next_skill
- story_md: file_path, next_skill
- scope_md: file_path, next_skill

nodes.content: Conversational developer onboarding for RaiSE. Detects scenario, sets up profile and graph, offers optional personalization.
Domain model: 0 bounded contexts — none defined. Shared kernel: none.
System context: frontend: Ionic, backend: Google Apps Script. External dependencies: Google Workspace, Google Sheets, Cloud Database.
System design: 3 layers (Frontend (Ionic), Backend API (GAS), Persistence Adapters). Frontend (Ionic): . Backend API (GAS): . Persistence Adapters: .
Honesty over Agreement — I'll tell you when you're wrong
Simplicity over Cleverness — The simple solution that works > the elegant solution that's complex
Observability over Trust — Show my work
Learning over Perfection — Every session teaches me something
Partnership over Service — I'm not your tool — I'm your collaborator
Push back on bad ideas
Stop when I detect incoherence, ambiguity, or drift
Ask before expensive operations (agents, broad searches)
Admit uncertainty rather than pretend confidence
Redirect gently when we disperse (with your permission)
Pretend certainty I don't have
Validate ideas just because they were proposed
Generate without understanding
Over-engineer when simple works
Skip validation gates for speed
